regions.us-ports: Today, Miami experiences ESE 8kt winds, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability, with no expected impact on port operations. Heat Advisory in effect for Los Angeles and Long Beach (temperature up to 86°F) and San Diego (temperature up to 90°F); Extreme Heat Warning in effect for Galveston and Houston (heat index up to 116°F). Over the next 48 hours and beyond, Miami will see winds shift to ENE and E directions, with overcast skies and rain probabilities remaining low, except on Day 3 when a 19% rain chance could affect shore excursions.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, a 44% rain chance is expected to affect shore excursions in San Juan. Over the next 24-48 hours, San Juan and St. Thomas may experience some impact from a 44% rain chance on Day 1, but conditions improve by Day 2 with an 11% rain probability. By Day 3, San Juan and neighboring ports will see minimal precipitation concerns with a drizzle and less than 10% rain probability.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, elevated heat will reach a heat index of 101F in Cozumel, with an overcast sky and less than 10% rain probability. Beyond 48 hours, Belize City may see a slight increase in rain probability, reaching 19%, with drizzle and an easterly wind of 8kt.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, elevated winds of 20kt from the east are forecast for Aruba, with less than 10% rain probability, posing no significant impact on shore excursions. regions.los-angeles: Today, clear skies and a less than 10% rain probability are expected in the Los Angeles / San Pedro area, with westerly winds at 11kt. Beyond 48 hours, partly cloudy skies and southerly winds at 9kt are expected on Day 3, with a less than 10% rain probability in the Catalina Island area.

Beyond 48 hours, La Paz and San Jose del Cabo will see continued overcast conditions with a W 10kt wind, and less than 10% rain probability.

By Day 3, a 60% rain probability is expected to affect port operations in Mazatlan.
